title: Distributional output mode for intermediate pipeline agents
description: Add a `preserve_distribution=True` flag to the sub-agent invocation layer (sub_agent.py) that, when set, appends a prompt suffix instructing the intermediate agent to return N alternatives with brief confidence rationale rather than a single collapsed answer. Downstream agents that consume this output receive a richer signal and can apply their own selection logic rather than inheriting the upstream mode.
rationale: The note identifies a precise mechanism: RLHF trains models to suppress distributional knowledge at output time, so every A2A hop is a mode-collapse step. By the time a multi-hop pipeline terminates, only the most RL-reinforced path survives — not the most accurate one. Mira's writer and researcher pipelines chain multiple sub-agent calls (plan → write → review). Each call currently asks for a single best output. This is exactly the architecture the note warns about: each hop narrows, compounds, and the final output reflects accumulated mode-collapse rather than the actual best answer.
diff_description: In sub_agent.py, add an optional parameter `preserve_distribution: bool = False` to the main invocation function. When True, append to the task prompt: '\n\nIMPORTANT: Do not collapse to a single answer. Return 2–3 candidate responses ranked by confidence. For each, add one sentence explaining what uncertainty or tradeoff it resolves. Label them OPTION_1, OPTION_2, OPTION_3.' In prompts.py, add a DISTRIBUTION_SUFFIX constant with this text so it can be reused. Enable this flag in the writer pipeline's plan-generation step (the highest-leverage point, since plan choice gates all downstream content) by passing preserve_distribution=True when dispatching the plan sub-agent. Leave write and review steps at default (single output) to avoid compounding verbosity downstream.
